---
title: "Customization Guide"
subtitle: "Adapt the Codex Version to Your Workflow"
---

# Overview

The Codex-native Clo-Author is customized through four active surfaces:

| Surface | Location | Use It For |
|---------|----------|------------|
| Constitution | `AGENTS.md` | Always-loaded rules, project state, core principles |
| Plugin manifest | `plugins/clo-author/.codex-plugin/plugin.json` | Plugin identity and discovery metadata |
| Skills | `plugins/clo-author/skills/` | Multi-step workflows |
| Active critic prompts | `plugins/clo-author/references/active-critics/` | Detailed live paired-critic prompt layer |
| References | `plugins/clo-author/references/` | Field calibration, workflow rules, role guidance |

# 1. Customize `AGENTS.md`

This is the one file Codex should always read.

Keep it lean:

- project metadata
- core principles
- file locations
- compile commands
- active reference pointers
- current project state

Do not bury long rubrics or role prompts in `AGENTS.md`. Those belong in plugin references.

If you are converting the template into a live project, this is also where you replace the shipped placeholders and current-state stubs.

# 2. Customize the Domain Profile

Edit `plugins/clo-author/references/domain-profile.md` for:

- field and subfields
- target journal tiers
- common datasets
- identification strategies
- notation conventions
- seminal references
- referee concerns

This is the main calibration layer for economics and adjacent disciplines.

# 3. Extend Journal Calibration

Edit `plugins/clo-author/references/journal-profiles.md` when you want journal-specific review culture. The `review` skill uses it when the user asks for journal-calibrated peer review.

# 4. Bootstrap the Template into a Working Project

Use the template defaults as a starting point, then fill in:

- project metadata in `AGENTS.md`
- the current project state table in `AGENTS.md`
- the field calibration in `plugins/clo-author/references/domain-profile.md`
- the first saved spec, plan, and session log under `quality_reports/`

The goal is to preserve the same architecture while swapping template defaults for project facts.

# 5. Add or Refine Skills

Each workflow lives in `plugins/clo-author/skills/[name]/SKILL.md`.

Use skills when:

- the workflow is multi-step
- the same sequence comes up repeatedly
- the workflow benefits from named roles or references
- the outputs should always be saved to a known location

Use the template in `templates/skill-template.md` to add new skills.

# 6. Decide What Stays Always-Loaded

A good rule of thumb:

- put stable, high-level instructions in `AGENTS.md`
- put detailed procedures in `plugins/clo-author/references/`
- put reusable workflow logic in skills

The active Codex references for most projects should include workflow, content standards, scoring, logging, domain calibration, and journal calibration.
Add `plugins/clo-author/references/content-invariants.md` when you want a hard numbered rule layer that critics can cite directly.

# 7. Handle Legacy Claude Assets Carefully

The repository still contains `.claude/` plus archived prompt copies under:

- `plugins/clo-author/references/legacy-agents/`
- `plugins/clo-author/references/legacy-rules/`
- `plugins/clo-author/hooks/legacy/`

Treat those as source material, not as the live Codex runtime. The live detailed critic layer is now `plugins/clo-author/references/active-critics/`.

# 8. Refresh Infrastructure Without Replacing the Project

When you want newer template mechanics:

- refresh `plugins/clo-author/skills/`, `plugins/clo-author/references/`, `templates/`, and guide docs
- compare against `https://github.com/hugosantanna/clo-author` as the canonical upstream
- preserve `paper/`, `scripts/`, `data/`, `quality_reports/`, `MEMORY.md`, and project-specific calibration edits
- treat infrastructure refresh as a controlled maintenance task, not a content rewrite

# 9. Persistence and Recovery

This repo assumes explicit persistence:

- plans in `quality_reports/plans/`
- specs in `quality_reports/specs/`
- decision records in `quality_reports/decisions/`
- session logs in `quality_reports/session_logs/`
- memory in `MEMORY.md`

If you customize the workflow, preserve these storage conventions so the project stays resumable across sessions.
